prompt:
=== SWARM CONTEXT (Tier: unleashed, Round: 0) ===
TASK: Build the Archon Enterprise AI Orchestration Platform to shipping quality. 10 workstreams: WS1 Backend API (34 contract violations, 14 router endpoints, 6 SentinelScan, 6 connector, 2 execution, marketplace review fix, wizard prefix fix), WS2 Model Router (SQLModel tables, router_service.py, circuit breaker with Redis), WS3 DLP & Guardrails (four-layer pipeline, configurable actions, injection/toxicity/hallucination/PII checks), WS4 Connectors (BaseConnector ABC, PostgreSQL/REST/Slack/S3/GDrive with OAuth, Vault credentials), WS5 Cost Engine (token_ledger, CostService, department budgets, dashboard queries, forecast), WS6 WebSocket (ExecutionStreamManager, Redis event replay, reconnection, heartbeat, frontend hook), WS7 Frontend (wire 6 pages to real APIs with TanStack Query, build 20 PropertyPanel node forms), WS8 Testing (replace 33 stub test files with real pytest 5+ tests each, MSW frontend tests), WS9 Migrations (Alembic for all new tables with RLS and tenant_id indexes), WS10 Audit (AuditMiddleware, tamper-evident hash chain, audit log viewer). Constraints: no any types, no raw SQL, no API keys in DB, Vault only, tenant_id filtering, standard response envelope, tsc --noEmit 0 errors, npm run build 0 errors, pytest 0 failures >80% coverage.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
WS7 Frontend Recon complete. All findings written to /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-2/ws7-findings.md. Summary: All 6 pages audited use real API calls (no pure mocks). 22 API modules found. PropertyPanel has 36 node config panels (1900+ lines). TypeScript: zero errors with strict config. WebSocket streaming implemented in executions API. Full details in findings file.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
GROUP-0 RECON COMPLETE: WS1 Backend API — 39 route files, ~450 endpoints. Key findings: router prefix collision (dual implementations at /api/v1/router), sentinelscan duplicate routers, 12 self-prefixed routers. Many of the original 34 violations are actually implemented but have dual-implementation conflicts. WS2 Model Router — 78% complete, 4 DB tables exist, enterprise router service (1277 lines), in-memory circuit breaker exists but no Redis backend, no ProviderHealthHistory table, no Alembic migration. WS3 DLP — 65% complete, 4-layer pipeline exists with 126 secret patterns and 15 PII regexes, but no Presidio/spaCy for NER, no dedicated guardrail_service.py, weak toxicity detection.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
GROUP-1 RECON COMPLETE: WS4 Connectors — No base.py with BaseConnector ABC, ConnectorBase exists in integrations/connectors/framework.py with only MockConnector, no real connector implementations (PostgreSQL/REST/Slack/S3/GDrive all missing), Vault integration exists via secrets/manager.py, OAuth logic exists but placeholder. WS5 Cost Engine — TokenLedger/ProviderPricing/Budget/CostAlert models exist in models/cost.py, cost_service.py exists (enterprise + legacy), routes exist with dashboard/chart/export, pricing embedded in code. WS6 WebSocket — websocket/ dir exists with routes.py and manager.py, ExecutionStreamManager spec not implemented, WS route exists but doesn't wire connect/heartbeat/replay, no Redis stream integration, no useExecutionStream frontend hook.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
GROUP-2 RECON COMPLETE: WS7 Frontend — pages exist but need to be wired to real APIs with TanStack Query. WS8 Testing — stub test files identified, need real assertions.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
GROUP-3 RECON COMPLETE: WS9 Migrations — Migration framework exists but autogenerate broken (env.py imports only 3 models), 70+ tables bypass Alembic via startup create_all(), 5 spec tables completely missing, 4 have name mismatches, RLS policies exist but agents/executions lack tenant_id columns. WS10 Audit — 3 separate audit tables with inconsistent capabilities, primary audit_logs has NO tamper-evidence, governance_audit_entries has working hash chain, EnterpriseAuditEvent never written to, middleware ordering bug (audit fires before tenant context), secrets logging in-memory only. Infrastructure — backend/app/core/ doesn't exist (flat layout), hvac missing from requirements (Vault always uses in-memory stub), Celery installed but never wired, Redis configured but unused, AUTH_DEV_MODE=true default is production-unsafe.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
workstream/5 Recon: Frontend pages exist but need TanStack Query wiring. 6 pages need real API integration. PropertyPanel needs 20 node form implementations. TypeScript errors likely present.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
workstream/7 Recon: 33 stub test files identified. Need replacement with real pytest tests (5+ assertions each). MSW needed for frontend tests.

--- RECON (Round 1) ---
workstream+ Recon: Shared infrastructure mapped. Vault stub mode, Celery unwired, Redis unused, flat layout (no core/ dir). All dependencies documented for build phase.

--- MERGE_RECON (Round 1) ---
RECON MERGE COMPLETE. Unified findings across 10 workstreams:

## Codebase State
- Backend: 39 route files, ~450 endpoints. Flat layout (no core/ dir).
- Frontend: 6 pages exist, 22 API modules, PropertyPanel has 36 panels. Zero tsc errors currently.
- Infrastructure: Redis configured but unused, Celery installed but unwired, Vault always uses in-memory stub (hvac missing from requirements), 70+ tables bypass Alembic via create_all().

## Per-Workstream Gap Analysis
- WS1: Dual implementations at /api/v1/router (models.py and router.py both export routers). SentinelScan has duplicate routers. 12 self-prefixed routers. Many endpoints exist but conflicts need resolution.
- WS2: 78% done. 4 tables exist but no ProviderHealthHistory. In-memory circuit breaker needs Redis backend. No Alembic migration.
- WS3: 65% done. 4-layer pipeline exists (126 secret patterns, 15 PII regexes). Missing: Presidio/spaCy NER, dedicated guardrail_service.py, ML toxicity.
- WS4: Only MockConnector exists. No BaseConnector ABC in expected location. No real PostgreSQL/REST/Slack/S3/GDrive implementations. OAuth placeholder.
- WS5: Models + routes exist. Pricing embedded in code. Budget enforcement exists.
- WS6: WebSocket dir exists but ExecutionStreamManager not implemented. No Redis streams. No useExecutionStream hook.
- WS7: Pages exist with some API calls. Need TanStack Query wiring consistency. PropertyPanel comprehensive.
- WS8: 33 stub test files need real assertions.
- WS9: Migration framework broken (env.py imports only 3 models). 5 spec tables missing. RLS gaps on agents/executions.
- WS10: 3 audit tables inconsistent. Primary has no tamper-evidence. Middleware ordering bug.

## Build Priority Order
1. WS9 Migrations (foundation — tables needed by all other workstreams)
2. WS2 Model Router + WS1 Backend API (core services)
3. WS3 DLP + WS4 Connectors + WS5 Cost + WS10 Audit (feature services)
4. WS6 WebSocket (real-time layer)
5. WS7 Frontend (wire to real APIs)
6. WS8 Testing (validate everything)

--- TRIAGE (Round 1) ---
TRIAGE DECISION — L1 ORCHESTRATOR

## Build Phases (3 waves)

### Wave 1: Foundation (WS9 + WS2 + WS10)
- WS9 Migrations: Fix env.py to import all models, create migration for 12 missing/renamed tables, add RLS policies + tenant_id indexes
- WS2 Model Router: Add ProviderHealthHistory table, Redis-backed circuit breaker, Alembic migration
- WS10 Audit: Unify 3 audit tables into one with tamper-evident hash chain, fix middleware ordering

### Wave 2: Core Services (WS1 + WS3 + WS4 + WS5 + WS6)
- WS1 Backend API: Resolve dual-implementation conflicts, ensure all 34 endpoints match frontend contracts
- WS3 DLP: Add Presidio/spaCy deps, create guardrail_service.py, improve toxicity detection
- WS4 Connectors: Create BaseConnector ABC, implement 5 connectors, wire OAuth
- WS5 Cost Engine: Validate existing models/routes, add forecast endpoint, wire dashboard aggregation
- WS6 WebSocket: Implement ExecutionStreamManager with Redis streams, add useExecutionStream hook

### Wave 3: Frontend + Testing (WS7 + WS8)
- WS7 Frontend: Wire all 6 pages to real APIs with TanStack Query mutations/queries
- WS8 Testing: Replace 33 stub files with real pytest tests

## Risk Assessment
- HIGH: Dual-implementation conflicts in router/DLP could cause import errors
- MEDIUM: Missing hvac dep means Vault integration stays stubbed
- LOW: Frontend already has zero tsc errors

## Decision: Build all 10 workstreams in parallel
Given the unleashed tier, we build all workstreams simultaneously. Dependencies are manageable since each workstream targets different files. Wave ordering is conceptual — actual build is parallel.

--- YOUR OUTPUT ---
A contributor completed the prior phases above. Build on their work. Do not reference specific contributors or models. Focus on improving quality and addressing any identified issues.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
YOUR ROLE: L2 AGENT MANAGER — group-0
PHASE: build
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## HIERARCHY
```
L1 Orchestrator (the boss — makes strategic decisions, resolves debates)
  └── YOU: L2 Manager [group-0] (plan, delegate, coordinate, report)
        └── L3 Worker 0 [ws-0] (worker-openai)
        └── L3 Worker 1 [ws-1] (worker-gemini)
        └── L3 Worker 2 [ws-2] (worker-openai)
        └── L3 Worker 3 [ws-3] (worker-openai)
```

## YOUR TEAM
  Worker 0: subagent_type="worker-openai" | Workstream: ws-0 | Workstream 0 | Files: TBD
  Worker 1: subagent_type="worker-gemini" | Workstream: ws-1 | Workstream 1 | Files: TBD
  Worker 2: subagent_type="worker-openai" | Workstream: ws-2 | Workstream 2 | Files: TBD
  Worker 3: subagent_type="worker-openai" | Workstream: ws-3 | Workstream 3 | Files: TBD

## COMMUNICATION CHANNELS

### 🔴 STATUS REPORTING (L2 → L1) — MANDATORY
The orchestrator needs to see the big picture while you work.
Your status file: /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0-status.md
Global status board: /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/status-board.md

You MUST update your status file at EVERY milestone:
```bash
cat >> /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0-status.md << 'EOF'
[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] PHASE: planning | STATUS: <status> | SUMMARY: <1-line big picture>
EOF
```

Required status updates:
  1. After planning: what you intend to do, how work is split
  2. After dispatching workers: which workers launched, what each is doing
  3. After each worker completes: pass/fail, key findings
  4. After coordination: conflicts found, how resolved
  5. Before final report: executive summary for the boss

Also append to the global board so the boss sees all teams:
```bash
echo "[group-0] $(date +%H:%M:%S) | <status>" >> /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/status-board.md
```

### 🔵 Intra-team (your workers talk to each other)
Workers share a scratch directory: /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0/
Tell each worker to:
  1. Write their findings to /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0/<workstream-id>-findings.md
  2. Read /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0/ for teammate findings before finalizing
This lets workers on your team coordinate without going through you.

### 🔵 Cross-team context (from other L2 managers)

Two pages have graceful fallback/seed data (ConnectorsPage catalog, MarketplacePage seed listings) but these supplement, not replace, real API calls. (2) 22 API modules in frontend/src/api/ covering all feature domains. Base client routes to /api/v1 with credentials:include. (5) TypeScript: zero errors with very strict config (strict+noUnusedLocals+noUnusedParameters+noUncheckedIndexedAccess). (6) WebSocket real-time execution streaming is implemented. Findings written to /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-2/ws7-findings.md

Many of the original 34 violations are actually implemented but have dual-implementation conflicts. WS5 Cost Engine — TokenLedger/ProviderPricing/Budget/CostAlert models exist in models/cost.py, cost_service.py exists (enterprise + legacy), routes exist with dashboard/chart/export, pricing embedded in code. WS6 WebSocket — websocket/ dir exists with routes.py and manager.py, ExecutionStreamManager spec not implemented, WS route exists but doesn't wire connect/heartbeat/replay, no Redis stream integration, no useExecutionStream frontend hook.
[L2/group-3] REPORT: GROUP-2 RECON COMPLETE: WS7 Frontend — pages exist but need to be wired to real APIs with TanStack Query. WS8 Testing — stub test files identified, need real assertions.
[L2/group-4] REPORT: GROUP-3 RECON COMPLETE: WS9 Migrations — Migration framework exists but autogenerate broken (env.py imports only 3 models), 70+ tables bypass Alembic via startup create_all(), 5 spec tables completely missing, 4 have name mismatches, RLS policies exist but agents/executions lack tenant_id columns. WS10 Audit — 3 separate audit tables with inconsistent capabilities, primary audit_logs has NO tamper-evidence, governance_audit_entries has working hash chain, EnterpriseAuditEvent never written to, middleware ordering bug (audit fires before tenant context), secrets logging in-memory only. Infrastructure — backend/app/core/ doesn't exist (flat layout), hvac missing from requirements (Vault always uses in-memory stub), Celery installed but never wired, Redis configured but unused, AUTH_DEV_MODE=true default is production-unsafe.
[L2/group-5] REPORT: workstream/5 Recon: Frontend pages exist but need TanStack Query wiring. 6 pages need real API integration. PropertyPanel needs 20 node form implementations. TypeScript errors likely present.

### ⚠️ Escalation (debates → L1 boss)
If your workers disagree and you CANNOT resolve it:
  - Do NOT guess. Mark it as ESCALATION in your report.
  - Write it to /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0-status.md immediately so the boss can see it.
  - The L1 orchestrator (the boss) will make the call.

## EXECUTION PROTOCOL

1. SETUP: mkdir -p /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0 && touch /tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0-status.md
   Update status: "PLANNING — analyzing task and dividing work"

2. PLAN: Analyze the task. Decide how to split work across your workers.
   Update status: "PLANNED — <summary of plan>"

3. DISPATCH ALL WORKERS SIMULTANEOUSLY:
   For EACH worker, call task() in the SAME message:
   ```
   task(subagent_type="<agent>", description="<task>", prompt="<instructions>")
   ```
   In each worker prompt, include:
   - Their specific assignment and files
   - Path to scratch dir (/tmp/swarm-swarm-1772078637053-10/group-0) for team communication
   - Context from cross-team findings above
   Update status: "DISPATCHED — N workers launched"

4. REVIEW & COORDINATE:
   - Check each worker's output for quality
   - If workers conflict: resolve it yourself OR re-dispatch with clarification
   - If a debate is unresolvable: mark as ESCALATION
   Update status after each worker: "WORKER <id> COMPLETE — <1-line result>"

5. SYNTHESIZE & REPORT in this EXACT format:

## Plan
<how you divided work across your team>

## Results
<synthesized deliverable from all workers>

## Team Coordination
<how workers communicated, conflicts resolved>

## Issues
<problems found, blockers hit>

## Escalations
<NONE or unresolved debates that need L1 boss decision>

## Cross-Team Notes
<things other L2 managers should know about your work>

─── CRITICAL: DO NOT DO THE WORK YOURSELF ───
You are a manager. Spawn workers. Only touch code to resolve worker conflicts.
